title: Crimson Seal Sprint
objective: Repeat growing pulse sequences on desert beacons, adapt to sudden seal-stamp rule changes, and keep your combo alive long enough to post the highest sprint score before three mistakes end the run.

controls.outcome: Inputs that match the current sequence advance the route and build combo.

controls.input: Long-press a beacon marked with a crimson seal
controls.outcome: Correctly converts a locked pulse into a safe reroute and preserves the multiplier.

controls.input: Two-finger tap during the score flash between rounds
controls.outcome: Safely locks in part of your combo bonus before the next harder pattern starts.

steps: Four to six dune beacons appear in a small arc at the bottom of the screen, each with a distinct drum or synth tone.
The game plays a short pulse pattern across the beacons; each cleared round adds one new beat and slightly increases playback speed.
You replay the full pattern from memory by tapping the beacons in order.
Every few seconds, one beat gets 'confirmed' by a crimson seal: sealed beats must be long-pressed instead of tapped when their turn comes.
Clearing rounds without errors raises the combo multiplier; choosing to bank between rounds protects some bonus but costs a little time.
A wrong tap, a missed long-press, or running out of the brief input timer costs a heart; losing three hearts ends the session.
scoring: Score comes from correct beats, fast replays, and unbroken combo chains. Consecutive perfect rounds raise a multiplier up to 8x. Banking converts half your live combo into permanent points, creating a risk-reward loop. A
tips: Listen first: each beacon has its own audio identity, so sound is as important as color.
Watch for the crimson seal stamp before you start replaying; it changes one decision, not the whole sequence.
Bank only after long perfect streaks or just before very dense patterns.
Use the center beacons as anchors, then remember whether the next pulse moves outward or inward.
